persona_A.comment: The product theory (shape × device × phase) catches bugs that are structurally impossible for shape-only tools—19/24 cross-domain bugs are provably uncatchable by PyTea. The custom Z3 UserPropagator for broadcasting is a genuine systems contribution: a domain-specific decision procedure integrated into DPLL(T) with eager propagation and conflict clause generation, not just an encoding trick.

persona_B.comment: The decidability proof for T_Π is non-trivial—it correctly identifies that T_device and T_phase are not stably infinite, so classical Nelson-Oppen fails, and invokes the Tinelli-Zarba extension for finite sorts. The NP-hardness result for reshape satisfiability via reduction from PARTITION is a clean complexity characterization. The contract discovery termination bound (|E| iterations) is modest but correctly proved.

persona_C.comment: There is no ML/LLM component—this is purely symbolic verification—but it targets the empirically validated #1 crash source in DL code (tensor shape errors). The cross-domain product theory goes beyond what any existing ML tooling provides, and the 91.7% recall on cross-domain bugs vs 54.2% for shape-only is a compelling practical result for ML practitioners. An ML-for-code researcher would find value in the problem framing and benchmark methodology, though the contribution is formal methods applied to ML, not ML methodology itself.

persona_D.comment: The tool solves a genuinely painful developer problem with sub-250ms verification time (fast enough for IDE integration) and actionable counterexample traces showing exact shape/device/phase at each computation step. The contract discovery loop automatically infers implicit constraints that developers leave undocumented, which is a concrete usability win over annotation-heavy alternatives.

persona_E.comment: The product theory T_Π provides clean algebraic unification: three decidable theories combined via Tinelli-Zarba with cross-sort axioms, a compositional shape transfer function algebra over the computation graph FSTS, and an extensible design (the paper explicitly sketches T_dtype and T_grad as future product factors). The UserPropagator-based broadcast theory is a well-motivated domain-specific theory plugin that participates in the same algebraic combination framework.
